nodes.label: Tests for ``_parse_guardrails`` and helpers — spec-load behavior for the policy
Parse YAML text using the spec's custom loader.      Needed so ``on:``, ``off:``
Empty guardrails block parses to a GuardrailsSpec     with default ask_timeout a
`ask_timeout: 0` → fail-loud at spec load (POLICIES.md §13).      A zero timeout
Non-integer ask_timeout → loud error (no silent coercion).
Full-schema dict: initial + values both land.
`{values: [...]}` without initial —     label is unset until a policy writes it
`integrity: {}` → typo guard (POLICIES.md §13).      An empty dict declaring nei
Bare-string `function:` path → FunctionRef with no arguments.
`handler:` is accepted as an alias for `function:`.      The proto/service-polic
Function policy without `function:` field → loud error.
Policies land in the list in their YAML declaration     order — the engine itera
`type: weird` → clear error listing the accepted value.
Every policy must declare `type:` — the dispatcher     uses it to pick the concr
A policy may override the spec-wide `ask_timeout:` via     its own field — valid
Per-policy `ask_timeout: 0` → same §13 rejection as     spec-level: the zero-is-
Write a full config.yaml that exercises the     guardrails block alongside other
AgentSpec.guardrails is None when the block is absent —     runtime builds a no-
The custom ``_ConfigYamlLoader`` must NOT convert     ``on:`` into a boolean key
The narrowing must not break ``true`` / ``false`` —     other parts of the spec
``condition: {}`` is treated identically to an omitted     ``condition:`` field
Unquoted YAML ints / bools coerce to strings — labels     are always string-valu
`condition: [foo, bar]` → loud rejection. Only a dict     makes sense for a labe
